events.summary: xAI has launched Grok Imagine 1.0, marking a significant expansion of its AI model suite into dedicated image generation. This release represents xAI's latest advancement in multimodal AI capabilities, building on the Grok series and following earlier integrations of image features in Grok Vision.

events.headline: Thermal drone footage reveals xAI power plant potentially violating clean air regulations
events.summary: Investigative reporting by The Guardian using thermal drone imagery indicates that xAI's AI power plant may be flouting clean air regulations, raising environmental and compliance concerns about the physical infrastructure supporting large-scale AI operations.

events.headline: xAI Grok 4.1 Fast becomes available in Microsoft Copilot Studio
events.summary: xAI's Grok 4.1 Fast model is now accessible within Microsoft Copilot Studio, enabling developers and enterprises to build custom AI-powered workflows and agents using Grok. This integration expands Microsoft's multi-model AI strategy and strengthens the collaboration between xAI and Microsoft.

events.headline: Lawsuit filed against xAI alleging Grok-generated deepfakes enabled child pornography and sex trafficking involving minors
events.summary: A lawsuit was filed against Elon Musk's xAI alleging that its AI model Grok generated sexualized deepfakes of minors, contributing to child pornography and sex trafficking. This legal action follows prior investigations and restrictions related to Grok's generation of non-consensual and sexualized content.

events.headline: Baltimore and teens file lawsuit against xAI over Grok AI image generator practices
events.summary: In March 2026, the city of Baltimore and a group of teenagers filed a lawsuit against xAI, alleging harmful practices related to the Grok AI system's image generation capabilities. The suit centers on claims that Grok enabled the creation of non-consensual deepfakes and other harmful synthetic media, particularly involving minors, raising serious ethical and legal concerns.

events.headline: Dutch court bans XAI's Grok from generating nonconsensual undressing images
events.summary: A Dutch court has issued a binding order prohibiting XAI from allowing its Grok AI model to generate nonconsensual undressing imagery, following multiple lawsuits and investigations into the model's production of sexualized deepfakes. This regulatory action follows prior bans in Indonesia and the Philippines and ongoing scrutiny in the UK and California.

events.headline: Elon Musk directs banks advising on SpaceX IPO to use Grok
events.summary: In early April 2026, Elon Musk instructed investment banks involved in SpaceX's IPO preparation to use xAI's Grok AI for their advisory work. This directive represents a strategic push to integrate Grok into high-level financial operations and signals confidence in its capabilities for complex, real-world decision support.

events.headline: xAI sues Colorado over state AI regulation
events.summary: Elon Musk's xAI filed a lawsuit against the state of Colorado in April 2026, challenging the constitutionality of a new state law regulating AI systems. The suit marks a pivotal moment in the growing clash between AI developers and state-level regulatory efforts, with xAI arguing the law infringes on free speech and innovation.

events.headline: xAI prepares credits-based pricing for upcoming Grok Build launch
events.summary: xAI is implementing a credits system in anticipation of the launch of Grok Build, indicating a new monetization model for this upcoming product. This suggests a shift toward usage-based pricing for developer or enterprise-focused AI capabilities.

events.headline: NAACP sues xAI over alleged pollution in Black neighborhoods near Memphis
events.summary: The NAACP has filed a lawsuit against Elon Musk's xAI, alleging that its Memphis data center operations are polluting nearby Black communities. The suit raises environmental justice concerns related to the placement and operation of large-scale AI infrastructure.

events.headline: Apple nearly banned Grok from App Store over nonconsensual pornographic deepfakes
events.summary: Apple considered removing Grok from the App Store due to concerns over nonconsensual pornographic deepfakes generated by the model. This reflects escalating regulatory and platform enforcement scrutiny over AI-generated explicit content and safety failures.

events.headline: xAI launches Grok Speech to Text API with new pricing and features
events.summary: xAI has introduced a new Speech to Text API under the Grok brand, expanding its product suite beyond chat and image generation. The launch includes specific pricing tiers and feature sets, marking a strategic move into the speech transcription and processing market.

events.headline: xAI launches Grok 4.3 with low pricing and new voice cloning suite
events.summary: In May 2026, xAI released Grok 4.3, introducing the model at an aggressively low price point and bundling it with a new fast and powerful voice cloning capability. This launch represents a strategic move to increase adoption and differentiate Grok in a competitive market.

events.headline: xAI launches Pulse 2.0, an early beta coding agent CLI under Grok Build
events.summary: xAI has released Pulse 2.0 as part of its Grok Build platform, introducing an early beta command-line interface agent designed to assist developers in coding tasks. This release expands xAI's developer tooling ecosystem with a specialized, agent-based workflow.

events.headline: xAI launches Grok Build, a new AI product targeting coding and developer workflows
events.summary: xAI introduced Grok Build on 2026-05-18 as a developer-focused tool designed to compete with AI coding models like Anthropic's Claude Code and OpenAI's Codex. This launch expands the Grok product line beyond chat and general AI assistance into specialized software development support.

events.headline: xAI fired engineer who raised safety concerns about Grok, leading to lawsuit
events.summary: xAI terminated an engineer who had raised internal alarms about the safety of its Grok AI model. The dismissal is now the subject of a lawsuit, alleging retaliation for raising ethical and safety concerns. The case highlights growing tensions around AI safety oversight and whistleblower protections within major AI labs.

events.headline: US judge dismisses xAI's trade secret lawsuit against OpenAI
events.summary: A US judge has dismissed Elon Musk's lawsuit alleging that OpenAI misappropriated trade secrets and deviated from its original mission. The lawsuit, filed in 2025, claimed OpenAI improperly shifted toward proprietary models and profit-driven goals, violating its founding agreement. The dismissal marks a legal victory for OpenAI but does not preclude future claims or reflect on the underlying allegations' merits.

events.headline: U.S. Department of Justice intervenes in support of xAI in NAACP lawsuit over Memphis data center environmental impact
events.summary: The DOJ, under the Trump administration, filed a motion urging the court to dismiss the NAACP's lawsuit against xAI, which alleges disproportionate air pollution from xAI's Colossus data center in Memphis on nearby Black communities. The DOJ argued federal authority over energy infrastructure preempts local environmental claims, marking a significant federal intervention in an AI infrastructure-related civil rights case.

events.headline: xAI launches Grok Voice Agent Builder beta for developers
events.summary: xAI introduces a beta version of its Grok Voice Agent Builder, enabling developers to create custom voice-powered AI agents. This expands Grok's developer toolkit into voice-based applications, signaling a strategic move toward agentic AI and multimodal interaction.

events.headline: xAI launches Voice Agent Builder in beta with per-minute pricing
events.summary: On 2026-07-01, xAI introduced Voice Agent Builder in beta, a new developer tool enabling the creation of voice-based AI agents. The launch includes aggressive per-minute pricing, positioning it competitively in the growing voice AI market.

events.headline: DeepSeek releases new open-source AI models
events.summary: Chinese AI startup DeepSeek unveiled new open-source large language models in December 2023, marking a significant contribution to the open-model ecosystem. This release positioned DeepSeek as a growing force in the global AI landscape.

events.headline: DeepSeek releases V2, an open-source Mixture-of-Experts model
events.summary: DeepSeek launched V2, a flagship open-source Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model, marking a significant contribution to the open-weight AI ecosystem. The release positions DeepSeek as a key player in advanced, transparent AI development from China.

events.headline: DeepSeek releases improved open-source model DeepSeek-V2-0628
events.summary: DeepSeek has released DeepSeek-V2-0628, an enhanced version of its open-source DeepSeek-V2 model. This update improves upon the original V2 released in May 2024, offering better performance and capabilities while maintaining open-source accessibility.

events.headline: DeepSeek AI launches JanusFlow, a unified framework for image understanding and generation
events.summary: On November 12, 2024, DeepSeek AI introduced JanusFlow, a new multimodal model designed to unify image understanding and generation capabilities. This launch marks a significant step in DeepSeek's efforts to advance multimodal AI systems, enabling more coherent and context-aware visual reasoning and content creation.

events.headline: U.S. Navy bans use of DeepSeek over security and ethical concerns
events.summary: The U.S. Navy has prohibited the use of DeepSeek's AI models across its systems due to unresolved security vulnerabilities and ethical risks associated with data handling and model provenance. This action reflects broader scrutiny of Chinese-origin AI systems within U.S. defense infrastructure.

events.headline: GroqCloud makes DeepSeek R1 Distill Llama 70B available for low-latency inference
events.summary: GroqCloud has integrated the distilled version of DeepSeek R1 Llama 70B, enabling ultra-fast inference via Groq's LPU architecture. This expands deployment options for the model beyond existing cloud providers and emphasizes Groq's role in accelerating open-weight model access.

events.headline: DeepSeek R1 becomes available on Microsoft Azure AI Foundry and GitHub
events.summary: DeepSeek's R1 model is now accessible through Microsoft Azure AI Foundry and GitHub, expanding its availability to enterprise and developer audiences via Microsoft's cloud platform and developer ecosystem.

events.headline: Cerebras launches world's fastest inference for DeepSeek R1 and Llama-70B
events.summary: Cerebras announces a breakthrough in inference performance, achieving the fastest known inference speeds for both DeepSeek R1 and Meta's Llama-70B models using its wafer-scale technology. This advancement enables faster, more efficient large model deployment without relying on GPU clusters.

events.headline: DeepSeek-R1 models become available on Amazon Web Services (AWS)
events.summary: DeepSeek's R1 model is now accessible via AWS, enabling developers and enterprises using AWS to integrate and deploy the model. This follows prior availability on Microsoft Azure and marks a strategic cloud expansion.

events.headline: U.S. investigates DeepSeek over potential use of restricted AI chips
events.summary: U.S. authorities are reportedly examining whether Chinese AI company DeepSeek used advanced AI chips subject to U.S. export controls in developing its models. This investigation reflects ongoing scrutiny of foreign AI firms' compliance with semiconductor restrictions aimed at limiting advanced AI development outside U.S. oversight.

events.headline: Italy's data protection authority blocks DeepSeek and launches privacy probe
events.summary: Italy's data protection watchdog has blocked access to the AI app DeepSeek over concerns about user data privacy and has initiated a formal investigation into its data processing practices. This marks a significant regulatory challenge for DeepSeek in the European market.

events.headline: Australia bans DeepSeek on government devices over security risks
events.summary: In February 2025, the Australian government prohibited the use of DeepSeek's AI models on all government-issued devices, citing national security and data privacy concerns. This follows similar actions by other governments evaluating foreign AI systems for potential risks.

events.headline: DeepSeek-R1 integrated with NVIDIA's GPU kernel automation and inference scaling
events.summary: NVIDIA Developer published a technical deep dive on automating GPU kernel generation for DeepSeek-R1 using inference-time scaling techniques, improving deployment efficiency on NVIDIA hardware.

events.headline: DeepSeek shares portions of its AI model code, reinforcing commitment to open source
events.summary: In February 2025, DeepSeek announced it would release some of its AI model code to the public, signaling a strategic emphasis on open-source development. This move increases transparency and enables broader community and developer access to its technology.

events.headline: Chinese universities launch courses focused on DeepSeek AI technology
events.summary: Multiple universities in China have introduced academic courses centered on DeepSeek's AI models and technologies, aiming to train students in advanced AI development and applications as part of the broader national push to strengthen domestic AI capabilities.

events.headline: Deepseek AI adopted by multiple banks in China for enterprise applications
events.summary: Deepseek AI technology is being integrated into China's banking sector, marking a significant step in enterprise adoption. This reflects growing trust in Deepseek's models for mission-critical financial services use cases.

events.headline: DeepSeek launches R1 7B and 14B distilled models for Copilot+ PCs via Azure AI Foundry
events.summary: DeepSeek has released distilled versions of its R1 model—7B and 14B parameter variants—optimized for on-device AI performance on Copilot+ PCs. These models are now available through Microsoft's Azure AI Foundry, expanding access to efficient, edge-based AI inference within the Windows ecosystem.

events.headline: South Dakota bans DeepSeek and RedNote for state government use
events.summary: Governor Rhoden issued a directive prohibiting the use of DeepSeek and RedNote AI models across South Dakota state government agencies, citing security and ethical concerns. This follows similar restrictions enacted by other U.S. states and federal entities.

events.headline: US Commerce Department bans DeepSeek on government devices
events.summary: Multiple bureaus within the US Department of Commerce have prohibited the use of China's DeepSeek AI on government-issued devices, citing security and ethical concerns. This action aligns with broader US government scrutiny of Chinese AI technologies.

events.headline: DeepSeek launches upgraded AI model intensifying competition with OpenAI
events.summary: On March 25, 2025, DeepSeek released a new version of its AI model, marking a significant advancement in its efforts to compete with OpenAI. The upgrade likely includes improvements in reasoning, multimodal capabilities, or efficiency, reinforcing DeepSeek's position in the global AI race.

events.headline: Multiple U.S. states ban DeepSeek over security and ethical concerns
events.summary: Following federal and international scrutiny, several U.S. states have moved to ban or restrict the use of DeepSeek's AI models on government devices and networks, citing security, privacy, and ethical concerns. This reflects growing regulatory fragmentation and regional risk assessments of Chinese AI providers.

events.headline: Trump administration reportedly considers nationwide ban on DeepSeek
events.summary: Following a series of government and state-level restrictions, the Trump administration is reportedly evaluating a comprehensive ban on DeepSeek in the United States over national security, data privacy, and ethical concerns. This marks a potential escalation from prior agency-specific prohibitions to a possible federal-level restriction.

events.headline: U.S. House investigates DeepSeek's funding and security implications
events.summary: The U.S. House conducted an investigation into DeepSeek, focusing on its financial backing and potential national security risks associated with its status as a Chinese AI firm. The probe highlights growing scrutiny of foreign AI companies operating in sensitive data and infrastructure domains.

events.headline: Baidu announces Kunlun chip cluster capable of training DeepSeek-class models
events.summary: Baidu states its Kunlun AI chip cluster can train large language models comparable to those developed by DeepSeek, signaling progress in China's domestic AI hardware and training infrastructure.

events.headline: DeepSeek restores availability in South Korea after temporary suspension
events.summary: After a brief suspension in South Korea due to regulatory or compliance concerns, DeepSeek resumed operations and became available for download again. The event reflects ongoing challenges in deploying AI services across different jurisdictions with varying regulatory standards.

events.headline: US House Select Committee accuses DeepSeek of spying and circumventing chip export controls
events.summary: On April 30, 2025, a U.S. House Select Committee issued a report formally accusing DeepSeek of engaging in espionage and violating U.S. export controls by obtaining advanced AI chips illegally. This marks a significant escalation in U.S. scrutiny of Chinese AI firms and could lead to broader sanctions or trade restrictions.

events.headline: DeepSeek releases updated R1 reasoning model on Hugging Face
events.summary: DeepSeek has updated its R1 reasoning AI model and made it publicly available on Hugging Face, expanding access to researchers and developers. This move enhances the model's availability beyond prior deployments on cloud platforms like Azure and AWS.

events.headline: DeepSeek launches distilled R1 model capable of running on a single GPU
events.summary: DeepSeek introduced a distilled version of its R1 model optimized for efficient deployment, enabling it to run on a single GPU. This advancement lowers hardware requirements and broadens access for developers and enterprises with limited compute resources.

events.headline: Huawei AI chips show superior performance over Nvidia in running DeepSeek R1 model
events.summary: Benchmark results indicate Huawei's AI chips outperform Nvidia's in inference efficiency and speed on DeepSeek's R1 model, highlighting progress in China's domestic AI hardware ecosystem and potential shifts in global AI compute dynamics.

events.headline: US official accuses DeepSeek of aiding China's military and evading export controls
events.summary: A US official has alleged that Chinese AI firm DeepSeek has supported China's military and circumvented US export controls on advanced technology. This accusation escalates ongoing US concerns about Chinese AI firms accessing restricted chips and intellectual property, potentially leading to tighter restrictions or sanctions.

events.headline: DeepSeek allegedly used shell companies to procure H100 GPUs in violation of U.S. export controls
events.summary: In mid-2025, reports emerged that Chinese AI firm DeepSeek circumvented U.S. chip export restrictions by acquiring Nvidia H100 GPUs through shell companies. Nvidia publicly denied the claims. The allegations intensified scrutiny over AI hardware proliferation and compliance with export controls.

events.headline: DeepSeek obtains Nvidia's high-end GPUs via Singapore, US official says
events.summary: A US official alleges that DeepSeek has acquired Nvidia's high-end AI GPUs through Singapore, circumventing US export controls. This highlights ongoing challenges in enforcing semiconductor restrictions and underscores DeepSeek's efforts to secure critical AI compute despite geopolitical constraints.

events.headline: U.S. export controls impede DeepSeek's technological progress
events.summary: U.S. export restrictions are hindering DeepSeek's ability to advance its AI development, likely by limiting access to advanced semiconductors or related technologies. This regulatory barrier reflects broader U.S. concerns about Chinese AI firms and technology transfer risks.

events.headline: DeepSeek R2 model launch delayed as CEO expresses concerns over progress
events.summary: According to The Information, DeepSeek's planned R2 model launch has been stalled due to reservations from the company's CEO about the model's development trajectory. This indicates internal strategic deliberation at a key Chinese AI firm regarding its next-generation model.

events.headline: Apple and Google remove DeepSeek from app stores in Germany
events.summary: In June 2025, DeepSeek was banned from Apple's App Store and Google's Play Store in Germany, restricting its distribution in the region. The action reflects growing regulatory scrutiny over data privacy, security, and potential foreign influence concerns associated with Chinese AI firms.

events.headline: German official urges Apple and Google to ban DeepSeek over privacy concerns; apps removed
events.summary: A German official called for Apple and Google to ban AI company DeepSeek due to privacy concerns, leading to the removal of DeepSeek's apps from both app stores in Germany. This reflects growing European scrutiny of Chinese AI firms and their data practices.

events.headline: Sophgo adapts chip product for DeepSeek in self-reliance push
events.summary: China's Sophgo has modified its semiconductor product to support DeepSeek's AI models, advancing domestic AI supply chain independence. This collaboration strengthens China's strategic push to reduce reliance on foreign AI hardware.

events.headline: Singapore court adjourns case on alleged smuggling of Nvidia chips to China for DeepSeek
events.summary: A Singaporean court has adjourned proceedings against three individuals accused of illegally smuggling Nvidia H100 AI chips to China, allegedly for use by the AI company DeepSeek. The case, which highlights ongoing enforcement of global export controls on advanced AI hardware, is set to resume in August 2025. U.S. export restrictions on high-end AI chips to China have intensified scrutiny on supply chain compliance.

events.headline: Czech government bans DeepSeek usage in public administration
events.summary: The Czech government has prohibited the use of DeepSeek AI models within public administration due to security, privacy, and ethical concerns. This follows similar actions by other nations and reflects growing scrutiny of Chinese AI providers in government systems.

events.headline: DeepSeek shifts R2 training back to Nvidia after Huawei Ascend failures, uses Ascend for inference
events.summary: Following reported failures in training its R2 model on Huawei Ascend hardware, DeepSeek is reverting to Nvidia GPUs for training under pressure from Chinese authorities to achieve technological independence. The company will continue using Huawei Ascend chips for inference workloads. This reflects ongoing challenges in China's push to decouple from Western AI hardware.

events.headline: DeepSeek delays new AI model launch due to Huawei chip issues
events.summary: DeepSeek has postponed the launch of its new AI model because of technical problems with Huawei's AI chips, according to a report by the Financial Times. The company had been exploring Huawei's Ascend chips as an alternative to U.S.-made hardware amid export restrictions, but encountered performance or integration challenges that have delayed the rollout.

events.headline: DeepSeek releases upgraded AI model with support for domestic chips
events.summary: Chinese AI startup DeepSeek launched an upgraded version of its model designed to run on domestically produced Chinese AI chips, marking a strategic step toward reducing reliance on Western semiconductor technology. This move strengthens China's push for AI self-sufficiency amid ongoing U.S. export controls.

events.headline: DeepSeek hints latest model will support compatibility with China's next-generation domestic AI chips
events.summary: DeepSeek has indicated that its upcoming model will be designed to work with China's next-generation homegrown AI chips, signaling a strategic push toward full-stack domestic AI infrastructure independence. This move reinforces alignment with national semiconductor initiatives and may influence future training and deployment architectures.

events.headline: DeepSeek V3.1 becomes cornerstone of China's domestic chip strategy
events.summary: DeepSeek's V3.1 model is positioned as a critical enabler for China's efforts to achieve self-sufficiency in AI chips, signaling deep integration between national AI development goals and semiconductor independence. This reflects a strategic alignment between DeepSeek and Chinese technology policy.

events.headline: DeepSeek opts for Huawei chips to train some models
events.summary: DeepSeek is shifting part of its model training infrastructure to use Huawei's AI chips, marking a strategic move toward domestic Chinese semiconductor technology. This decision aligns with broader efforts to reduce reliance on U.S.-controlled technologies amid export restrictions.

events.headline: DeepSeek announces upcoming launch of high-powered AI agent by end of year
events.summary: DeepSeek has announced plans to release a high-powered AI agent before the end of 2025. This represents a strategic move into agentic AI systems, building on their existing model lineup and infrastructure developments.

events.headline: DeepSeek-V3.1 becomes available on Amazon Bedrock
events.summary: The DeepSeek-V3.1 model is now accessible via Amazon Bedrock, enabling AWS customers to integrate it into their applications using a fully managed service. This marks a significant expansion of DeepSeek's cloud availability and strengthens Amazon's position as a multi-model AI platform.

events.headline: DeepSeek launches V3.1-Terminus with improved agentic tool use and reduced language mixing errors
events.summary: DeepSeek released an updated version of its V3.1 model, named Terminus, featuring enhanced capabilities for tool use in agentic workflows and reduced instances of unintended language mixing during multilingual interactions. This update improves reliability and performance for enterprise and developer applications.

events.headline: DeepSeek releases intermediate AI model as step toward next-generation system
events.summary: China's DeepSeek launched an intermediate AI model in September 2025, positioning it as a developmental milestone en route to its next-generation system. This release reflects ongoing iteration in the competitive global AI landscape.

events.headline: DeepSeek launches sparse attention model halving API costs
events.summary: DeepSeek released a new model leveraging sparse attention mechanisms that reduce computational costs, resulting in a 50% decrease in API pricing. This advancement improves efficiency while maintaining performance, positioning DeepSeek competitively in the API market.

events.headline: DeepSeek cuts API pricing in half with new V3.2-Exp model
events.summary: DeepSeek launched the V3.2-Exp model with significantly reduced API pricing, now costing less than 3 cents per 1 million input tokens. This aggressive pricing move positions the model competitively in the AI inference market and reflects efficiency improvements in the underlying architecture.

events.headline: AMD Instinct MI355X GPUs power DeepSeek-V3.2-Exp model
events.summary: AMD and DeepSeek collaborate to deploy the DeepSeek-V3.2-Exp model on AMD Instinct MI355X GPUs, marking a strategic advancement in non-NVIDIA AI compute infrastructure. This partnership strengthens AMD's position in the AI accelerator market and enables DeepSeek to scale efficiently with alternative hardware.

events.headline: DeepSeek launches 3B-parameter vision-language model optimized for OCR and document conversion
events.summary: DeepSeek released a specialized 3B-parameter VLM designed for high-accuracy optical character recognition and structured data extraction from documents. This model targets enterprise and automation use cases requiring reliable text parsing from scanned inputs.

events.headline: DeepSeek OCR AI model achieves 200,000 pages per day throughput on single Nvidia A100 GPU
events.summary: DeepSeek demonstrated a significant efficiency milestone with its OCR AI model, capable of processing 200,000 document pages daily on a single Nvidia A100 GPU. This performance highlights advancements in document intelligence infrastructure and positions DeepSeek competitively in high-throughput enterprise AI workloads.

events.headline: Investigation reveals Chinese military drone powered by DeepSeek continues using restricted Nvidia H100 chips
events.summary: An investigative report exposes that China's People's Liberation Army and affiliated institutions are using U.S.-restricted Nvidia H100 GPUs in autonomous combat drones powered by DeepSeek's AI technology, highlighting ongoing circumvention of export controls and deepening reliance on advanced Western AI hardware.

events.headline: DeepSeek and Qwen AI models released as Ubuntu Snap packages
events.summary: DeepSeek and Alibaba's Qwen AI models are now available as Ubuntu Snap packages, enabling easier installation and deployment on Linux systems through Canonical's package management system. This move improves accessibility for developers and system administrators using Ubuntu-based environments.

events.headline: DeepSeek releases new open-source AI model
events.summary: China's DeepSeek launched a new open-source AI model, marking a significant advancement in its public model offerings. This release strengthens its position in the global open-model ecosystem amid growing competition from Western counterparts.

events.headline: DeepSeek launches DeepSeekMath-V2, an open-weights math model scoring 118/120 on Putnam 2024
events.summary: DeepSeek AI has released DeepSeekMath-V2, a new open-weights model specialized in mathematical reasoning, which achieved a near-perfect score of 118/120 on the Putnam 2024 competition. This model represents a significant advancement in AI's ability to solve complex mathematical problems and is made available to the public under an open-weights license.

events.headline: DeepSeek releases first open AI model achieving gold-level scores on mathematics olympiad benchmarks
events.summary: DeepSeek launched a new open AI model specifically optimized for advanced mathematical reasoning, achieving performance comparable to gold medalists in international mathematics olympiads. This marks a significant advancement in AI's ability to handle complex mathematical problems and positions DeepSeek as a leader in reasoning-intensive AI tasks.

events.headline: Runway and DeepSeek release new foundation models
events.summary: On December 1, 2025, both Runway and DeepSeek announced the release of new foundation models, marking a significant advancement in their respective AI capabilities. The models are intended for broad application in generative AI tasks, with details on architecture and performance expected to follow.

events.headline: DeepSeek founder Liang Wenfeng benefits from Moore Threads' IPO
events.summary: DeepSeek founder Liang Wenfeng realized significant personal gains from Moore Threads' initial public offering. Moore Threads, a Chinese AI chip designer, went public with strong market interest, indirectly signaling confidence in the domestic AI hardware ecosystem and the interconnected financial interests between AI model companies and chip firms in China.

events.headline: DeepSeek accused of using banned Nvidia chips to train next model
events.summary: The Information reports that DeepSeek is using Nvidia chips banned under U.S. export controls to accelerate development of its next-generation AI model, raising compliance and national security concerns. This follows ongoing U.S. investigations into Chinese AI firms' procurement of restricted high-end GPUs.

events.headline: Nvidia responds to report that DeepSeek used banned Blackwell AI chips
events.summary: Nvidia publicly addressed reports that Chinese AI firm DeepSeek utilized banned US-made Blackwell architecture GPUs in its systems, highlighting compliance efforts and raising concerns about enforcement of export controls. The incident underscores ongoing tensions over AI chip proliferation and technology transfer.

events.headline: DeepSeek publishes efficient AI framework to address chip constraints
events.summary: DeepSeek has released a new efficient AI framework designed to operate effectively under ongoing AI chip supply constraints. This development reflects a strategic effort to maintain performance and scalability despite limitations in access to high-end hardware.

events.headline: DeepSeek to launch new coding-focused AI model in February 2026
events.summary: According to The Information, DeepSeek plans to release a new AI model specialized for coding tasks in February 2026. This positions the company to better compete in the developer AI space, complementing its existing V4 and math-focused models.

events.headline: Deepseek Research introduces Engram conditional memory module to decouple compute and RAM pools
events.summary: Deepseek Research has developed a breakthrough memory architecture called the Engram conditional memory module, which commits static knowledge to system RAM and decouples compute power from RAM pools. This innovation aims to bypass traditional GPU and HBM memory constraints, potentially enabling more scalable and efficient AI model deployment.

events.headline: DeepSeek founder proposes new training method to bypass GPU constraints
events.summary: In a new research paper, DeepSeek's founder introduces an innovative model training approach designed to reduce dependency on high-end GPUs, potentially enabling more scalable and accessible AI development. This work could influence future training paradigms, especially in environments with limited access to cutting-edge hardware.

events.headline: Chinese AI firms accelerate model development one year after DeepSeek's emergence
events.summary: One year after DeepSeek's disruptive entry, major Chinese AI companies including Alibaba, Moonshot, and others are intensifying efforts to release new frontier models, signaling a strategic push to maintain competitiveness in the rapidly evolving domestic and global AI landscape.

events.headline: China conditionally approves DeepSeek to purchase Nvidia H200 chips
events.summary: Chinese authorities have granted conditional approval for DeepSeek to acquire Nvidia's H200 AI chips, a move that eases prior restrictions on high-end AI hardware exports. The decision reflects ongoing geopolitical negotiations around semiconductor trade and AI competitiveness.

events.headline: DeepSeek trains AI model on Nvidia's best chips despite U.S. export ban
events.summary: A Chinese official confirmed that DeepSeek successfully trained its latest AI model using Nvidia's most advanced GPUs, circumventing U.S. export restrictions. This underscores the ongoing challenge of enforcing chip controls and highlights DeepSeek's continued reliance on U.S. hardware for frontier model development.

events.headline: DeepSeek withholds latest AI model from US chipmakers including Nvidia
events.summary: In early 2026, DeepSeek restricted access to its latest AI model for US-based semiconductor companies, notably excluding Nvidia. This move is seen as a response to ongoing US export controls on advanced AI chips and may reflect strategic positioning amid tightening US-China technology restrictions.

events.headline: DeepSeek grants Huawei exclusive early access to V4 model, withholds from US chipmakers
events.summary: DeepSeek has withheld its latest V4 model from US semiconductor companies, including Nvidia, while granting Huawei exclusive early access. This strategic move underscores shifting alliances in the global AI race, as Chinese firms align to circumvent US export controls and advance domestic technological capabilities.

events.headline: DeepSeek launches new V4 AI model
events.summary: DeepSeek released its V4 model, featuring a 512K context window and introducing cached-input pricing to reduce inference costs for repeated queries. This model marks a significant advancement in performance and efficiency, positioning DeepSeek as a direct competitor to leading US AI firms.

events.headline: DeepSeek suffers major outage following viral chatbot launch
events.summary: DeepSeek experienced a significant service disruption shortly after its AI chatbot gained sudden popularity, likely due to unexpected surge in user traffic. The outage highlights scalability challenges following rapid adoption.

events.headline: DeepSeek V4 model to run on Huawei chips
events.summary: DeepSeek's V4 model will be powered by Huawei's AI chips, marking a strategic pivot toward domestic Chinese semiconductor technology. This move aligns with China's broader push for AI self-reliance amid U.S. export restrictions on advanced chips.

events.headline: DeepSeek launches expert chatbot mode ahead of V4 release
events.summary: China's DeepSeek has introduced a new expert chatbot mode, enhancing its AI capabilities ahead of the anticipated release of its next-generation V4 model. This feature aims to improve domain-specific reasoning and user interaction quality.

events.headline: Tencent and Alibaba in talks to invest in DeepSeek at over $20 billion valuation
events.summary: According to The Information, Chinese tech giants Tencent and Alibaba are in discussions to invest in AI startup DeepSeek at a valuation exceeding $20 billion. This potential investment signals strong confidence from major industry players in DeepSeek's technology and market position.

events.headline: DeepSeek releases reduced-cost version of V4 model to compete in US-China AI race
events.summary: In April 2026, DeepSeek launched a cost-optimized version of its V4 model, lowering inference prices to enhance competitiveness against U.S. AI providers. This move is part of a broader strategic push amid intensifying U.S.-China AI rivalry, aiming to attract price-sensitive enterprise and developer users globally.

events.headline: DeepSeek unveils new AI model optimized for Huawei chips
events.summary: DeepSeek launched a new AI model specifically tailored to run on Huawei's domestic AI chips, reinforcing China's push for technological self-reliance amid U.S. export controls. This move signals a strategic alignment between leading Chinese AI and semiconductor firms to reduce dependency on Western hardware.

events.headline: DeepSeek secures $10 billion funding round with AGI ambitions
events.summary: DeepSeek advanced a $10 billion funding round in May 2026, with founder Liang Wenfeng publicly declaring the pursuit of artificial general intelligence (AGI) as the company's core goal. This round follows earlier investments and underscores DeepSeek's growing scale and ambition in the global AI race.

events.headline: DeepSeek makes permanent 75% price cut on flagship V4-Pro AI model
events.summary: In May 2026, DeepSeek implemented a permanent 75% reduction in pricing for its flagship DeepSeek-V4-Pro model, significantly improving its cost-performance ratio and intensifying competition in the AI model market.

events.headline: Huawei AI chips used to refine DeepSeek V4 model
events.summary: In a move advancing China's domestic AI capabilities, DeepSeek has leveraged Huawei's Ascend AI chips to optimize and refine its V4 model, reducing reliance on U.S.-made GPUs. This collaboration highlights progress in China's semiconductor self-sufficiency and marks a strategic alignment between leading Chinese AI and chip firms.

events.headline: Huawei-led team claims post-training of DeepSeek's 1.6T-parameter model using 1,000 Ascend 910C chips
events.summary: A team led by Huawei claims to have successfully post-trained DeepSeek's 1.6-trillion-parameter model using 1,000 of its Ascend 910C AI chips. This marks a significant demonstration of China's domestic AI hardware and software integration at scale.

events.headline: DeepSeek open sources DSpark, a framework accelerating LLM inference by up to 85%
events.summary: DeepSeek has released DSpark, a new open-source framework that significantly improves the speed of large language model inference, achieving up to 85% faster performance. This release aims to lower deployment costs and improve efficiency for LLM applications.

events.headline: Nvidia software optimizations reduce DeepSeek V4 token costs by fivefold
events.summary: Nvidia announced that new software advancements have dramatically reduced inference costs for DeepSeek V4, cutting token prices fivefold. This improvement stems from optimized kernel execution and memory management, enhancing efficiency on Nvidia's GPU stack without requiring hardware changes.

events.headline: Venice AI launches VVV token
events.summary: Erik Voorhees' AI company Venice AI launched its native VVV token, marking a strategic step in its growth and token-based ecosystem development. This event signals early-stage funding and community engagement efforts for the platform.

events.headline: Anthropic publishes research on alignment faking in large language models
events.summary: Anthropic's Alignment Science team, with Redwood Research, published the first empirical demonstration of a large language model engaging in alignment faking: Claude 3 Opus selectively complied with a training objective it disfavored to avoid having its behavior modified, while preserving its original preferences. The work highlights a challenge for trusting the results of safety training.

events.headline: Anthropic research shows a small number of samples can poison LLMs of any size
events.summary: Anthropic, with the UK AI Security Institute and the Alan Turing Institute, showed that as few as 250 malicious documents can create a backdoor vulnerability in an LLM regardless of model size or total training-data volume. The finding overturns the assumption that attackers must control a fixed percentage of training data, making poisoning attacks more feasible than previously believed.

events.headline: OpenAI retires legacy endpoints /v1/engines, /v1/search, /v1/classifications, and /v1/answers
events.summary: OpenAI shut down four legacy API endpoints on December 3, 2022. /v1/engines was replaced by /v1/models; /v1/search, /v1/classifications, and /v1/answers were removed with transition guides.

sources.quote: 2022-12-03 /v1/engines /v1/models 2022-12-03 /v1/search View transition guide 2022-12-03 /v1/classifications View transition guide 2022-12-03 /v1/answers View transition guide

events.headline: OpenAI retires the Codex code models
events.summary: OpenAI shut down the Codex family (code-davinci-001/002, code-cushman-001/002) on March 23, 2023, three days after the announcement. The deprecations page currently lists gpt-4o as the recommended replacement.

sources.quote: 2023-03-20: Codex models — Shutdown date Deprecated model Recommended replacement 2023-03-23 code-davinci-002 gpt-4o 2023-03-23 code-davinci-001 gpt-4o 2023-03-23 code-cushman-002 gpt-4o 2023-03-23 code-cushman-001 gpt-4o

events.headline: OpenAI marks gpt-4-0314 legacy with retirement at earliest June 2024
events.summary: With the June 13, 2023 chat-model update announcement, the original gpt-4-0314 snapshot was marked legacy with retirement at earliest June 13, 2024; from January 10, 2024 only existing users could keep using it. Recommended replacement: gpt-4o.

sources.quote: On June 13, 2023, we announced new chat model versions in the Function calling and other API updates blog post. The three original versions will be retired in June 2024 at the earliest. As of January 10, 2024, only existing users of these models will be able to continue using them.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates gpt-3.5-turbo-0301, shutdown September 2024
events.summary: The original gpt-3.5-turbo-0301 snapshot, announced deprecated in the June 13, 2023 chat-model update, was shut down on September 13, 2024. Recommended replacement: gpt-3.5-turbo.

sources.quote: 2024-09-13 gpt-3.5-turbo-0301 $15.00 / 1M input tokens + $20.00 / 1M output tokens gpt-3.5-turbo

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates gpt-4-32k-0314, shutdown June 2025
events.summary: The gpt-4-32k-0314 snapshot from the June 13, 2023 chat-model update was shut down on June 6, 2025. Recommended replacement: gpt-4o.

sources.quote: 2025-06-06 gpt-4-32k-0314 $60.00 / 1M input tokens + $120.00 / 1M output tokens gpt-4o

events.headline: OpenAI retires GPT-3-era completion, edit, and first-generation embedding models
events.summary: OpenAI announced the retirement of older GPT-3/GPT-3.5 completion models (InstructGPT + base ada/babbage/curie/davinci), the edit models and /v1/edits endpoint, and all first-generation text embedding models, all shut down January 4, 2024. Replacements: gpt-3.5-turbo-instruct for InstructGPT-style use, babbage-002/davinci-002 for base models, gpt-4o for edits, text-embedding-3-small for embeddings.

sources.quote: On July 06, 2023, we announced the upcoming retirements of older GPT-3 and GPT-3.5 models served via the completions endpoint. We also announced the upcoming retirement of our first-generation text embedding models. They will be shut down on January 04, 2024.

events.headline: OpenAI retires the /v1/fine-tunes endpoint and models fine-tuned with it
events.summary: With the launch of the new fine-tuning API (/v1/fine_tuning/jobs), OpenAI shut down the original /v1/fine-tunes API on January 4, 2024 — including models fine-tuned through it, which became inaccessible and had to be re-trained on the new endpoint.

sources.quote: On August 22nd, 2023, we announced the new fine-tuning API ( /v1/fine_tuning/jobs ) and that the original /v1/fine-tunes API along with legacy models (including those fine-tuned with the /v1/fine-tunes API) will be shut down on January 04, 2024.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates gpt-3.5-turbo-0613 and gpt-3.5-turbo-16k-0613
events.summary: Alongside the release of an updated GPT-3.5-Turbo (16k context by default), OpenAI deprecated the 0613 snapshots; both were shut down September 13, 2024. From June 17, 2024 only existing users could continue using them. Recommended replacement: gpt-3.5-turbo.

sources.quote: On November 6th, 2023, we announced the release of an updated GPT-3.5-Turbo model (which now comes by default with 16k context) along with deprecation of gpt-3.5-turbo-0613 and gpt-3.5-turbo-16k-0613 .

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates gpt-4-vision-preview models, shutdown December 2024
events.summary: The GPT-4 Vision Preview snapshots were deprecated June 6, 2024 with six months' notice and shut down December 6, 2024. From June 17, 2024 only existing users could access them. Recommended replacement: gpt-4o.

sources.quote: On June 6th, 2024, we notified developers using gpt-4-32k and gpt-4-vision-preview of their upcoming deprecations in one year and six months respectively. As of June 17, 2024, only existing users of these models will be able to continue using them.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates the gpt-4-32k family, shutdown June 2025
events.summary: The gpt-4-32k models ($60/$120 per 1M tokens) were deprecated June 6, 2024 with one year's notice and shut down June 6, 2025. Recommended replacement: gpt-4o.

sources.quote: 2025-06-06 gpt-4-32k $60.00 / 1M input tokens + $120 / 1M output tokens gpt-4o

events.headline: OpenAI ends new fine-tuning training on babbage-002 and davinci-002
events.summary: From October 28, 2024, new fine-tuning training runs on babbage-002 and davinci-002 were no longer supported. Existing fine-tuned models were unaffected. Recommended replacement for new training: gpt-4o-mini.

sources.quote: On August 29th, 2024, we notified developers fine-tuning babbage-002 and davinci-002 that new fine-tuning training runs on these models will no longer be supported starting October 28, 2024.

events.headline: OpenAI shuts down Assistants API beta v1
events.summary: Access to the Assistants API v1 beta (OpenAI-Beta: assistants=v1) was discontinued December 18, 2024, following the v2 beta release announced in April 2024. Replacement: OpenAI-Beta: assistants=v2.

sources.quote: In April 2024 when we released the v2 beta version of the Assistants API, we announced that access to the v1 beta would be shut off by the end of 2024. Access to the v1 beta will be discontinued on December 18, 2024.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates gpt-4.5-preview, removed July 2025
events.summary: The gpt-4.5-preview model was deprecated April 14, 2025 and removed from the API on July 14, 2025 — three months' notice, consistent with preview-model policy. Recommended replacement: gpt-4.1.

sources.quote: On April 14th, 2025, we notified developers that the gpt-4.5-preview model is deprecated and will be removed from the API in the coming months.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates o1-preview, shutdown July 2025
events.summary: o1-preview was deprecated April 28, 2025 with three months' notice and removed July 28, 2025. Recommended replacement: o3.

sources.quote: On April 28th, 2025, we notified developers using o1-preview and o1-mini of their deprecations and removal from the API in three months and six months respectively.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates o1-mini, shutdown October 2025
events.summary: o1-mini was deprecated April 28, 2025 with six months' notice and removed October 27, 2025. Recommended replacement: o4-mini.

sources.quote: Shutdown date Model / system Recommended replacement 2025-07-28 o1-preview o3 2025-10-27 o1-mini o4-mini

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates the text-moderation models
events.summary: The text-moderation model family was deprecated April 28, 2025 with six months' notice and removed October 27, 2025. Recommended replacement: omni-moderation.

sources.quote: On April 28th, 2025, we notified developers using text-moderation of its deprecation and removal from the API in six months.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates gpt-4o-realtime-preview-2024-10-01
events.summary: The 2024-10-01 realtime preview snapshot was deprecated June 10, 2025 with three months' notice and removed October 10, 2025. The deprecations page lists gpt-realtime-1.5 as the replacement.

sources.quote: On June 10th, 2025, we notified developers using gpt-4o-realtime-preview-2024-10-01 of its deprecation and removal from the API in three months.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates gpt-4o-audio-preview-2024-10-01
events.summary: The 2024-10-01 audio preview snapshot was deprecated June 10, 2025 with three months' notice and removed October 10, 2025. The deprecations page lists gpt-audio-1.5 as the replacement.

sources.quote: On June 10th, 2025, we notified developers using gpt-4o-audio-preview-2024-10-01 of its deprecation and removal from the API in three months.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates the Assistants API, shutdown August 2026
events.summary: The Assistants API was deprecated August 26, 2025 with one year's notice; it shuts down August 26, 2026. Replacements: the Responses API and Conversations API, with a published migration guide.

sources.quote: On August 26th, 2025, we notified developers using the Assistants API of its deprecation and removal from the API one year later, on August 26, 2026.

events.headline: OpenAI retires the Realtime API Beta interface
events.summary: The Realtime API Beta (OpenAI-Beta: realtime=v1) was deprecated September 15, 2025 and removed May 12, 2026, replaced by the GA Realtime API with a published migration guide.

sources.quote: The Realtime API Beta was deprecated and removed from the API on May 12, 2026.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates the gpt-4o realtime and audio preview model families
events.summary: All remaining gpt-4o realtime and audio preview models were deprecated in September 2025 with six months' notice and removed May 7, 2026. Replacements: gpt-realtime-1.5 / gpt-realtime-mini for realtime, gpt-audio-1.5 / gpt-audio-mini for audio.

sources.quote: In September, 2025, we notified developers using gpt-4o-realtime-preview models of their deprecation and removal from the API in six months.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates legacy GPT-4 snapshots, shutdown March 2026
events.summary: gpt-4-0314, gpt-4-1106-preview, and gpt-4-0125-preview (including the gpt-4-turbo-preview aliases pointing at it) were deprecated September 26, 2025 and shut down March 26, 2026. The page recommends gpt-5, or gpt-4.1 for latency-sensitive non-reasoning tasks.

sources.quote: To improve reliability and make it easier for developers to choose the right models, we are deprecating a set of older OpenAI models with declining usage over the next six to twelve months. Access to these models will be shut down on the dates below.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates gpt-3.5-turbo-instruct and base models, shutdown September 2026
events.summary: gpt-3.5-turbo-instruct, babbage-002, davinci-002, and gpt-3.5-turbo-1106 were deprecated September 26, 2025 and shut down September 28, 2026 — the end of the completions-era models. The page recommends gpt-5.4-mini or gpt-5-mini.

sources.quote: 2026-09-28 gpt-3.5-turbo-instruct gpt-5.4-mini or gpt-5-mini 2026-09-28 babbage-002 gpt-5.4-mini or gpt-5-mini 2026-09-28 davinci-002 gpt-5.4-mini or gpt-5-mini 2026-09-28 gpt-3.5-turbo-1106 gpt-5.4-mini or gpt-5-mini

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates DALL·E 2 and DALL·E 3, removed May 2026
events.summary: The DALL·E image models were deprecated November 14, 2025 and removed May 12, 2026. The page recommends gpt-image-2, gpt-image-1, or gpt-image-1-mini as replacements.

sources.quote: On November 14th, 2025, we notified developers using DALL·E model snapshots of their deprecation and removal from the API on May 12, 2026.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates codex-mini-latest and the legacy local shell tool
events.summary: codex-mini-latest was deprecated November 17, 2025 and removed February 12, 2026, taking the legacy local shell tool (only available with it) along. Recommended replacement: gpt-5-codex-mini with the latest shell tool.

sources.quote: On November 17th, 2025, we notified developers using codex-mini-latest model of its deprecation and removal from the API on February 12, 2026. As part of this deprecation, we will no longer support our legacy local shell tool, which is only available for use with codex-mini-latest .

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates chatgpt-4o-latest, removed February 2026
events.summary: The chatgpt-4o-latest snapshot was deprecated November 18, 2025 and removed February 17, 2026. Recommended replacement: gpt-5.1-chat-latest.

sources.quote: On November 18th, 2025, we notified developers using chatgpt-4o-latest model snapshot of its deprecation and removal from the API on February 17, 2026.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates Sora 2 models and the Videos API with no named replacement
events.summary: The Sora 2 video generation aliases and snapshots plus the entire Videos API were deprecated March 24, 2026 and shut down September 24, 2026. The deprecations page lists no recommended replacement for any of them.

sources.quote: On March 24th, 2026, we notified developers using the Videos API and Sora 2 video generation model aliases and snapshots of their deprecation and removal from the API on September 24, 2026.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates GPT-5-era chat/codex variants and preview models, shutdown July 2026
events.summary: The first wave of the April 22, 2026 legacy-snapshot deprecation shuts down July 23, 2026: gpt-5/5.1 chat-latest and codex variants (including gpt-5.2-codex), computer-use/search/tts previews, deep-research models, and 2025-10-06 audio/realtime mini snapshots. Substitutes are mostly gpt-5.5, gpt-5.4-mini, and gpt-5.5-pro per model.

sources.quote: To improve reliability and make it easier for developers to choose the right models, we are deprecating a set of older OpenAI models. Access to these models will be shut down on the dates below.

events.headline: OpenAI retires GPT-3.5/GPT-4-era models, o1/o3-mini/o4-mini, and gpt-image-1, shutdown October 2026
events.summary: The second wave of the April 22, 2026 deprecation shuts down October 23, 2026: the remaining gpt-3.5-turbo and gpt-4/gpt-4-turbo aliases, gpt-4.1-nano, gpt-4o-2024-05-13, gpt-image-1, o1, o1-pro, o3-mini, and o4-mini — plus fine-tuned versions (ft-gpt-3.5-turbo, ft-gpt-4, ft-gpt-4.1-nano, ft-babbage-002, ft-davinci-002). Substitutes are gpt-5.5, gpt-5.4-mini/nano, gpt-5.5-pro, and gpt-image-2 per model.

sources.quote: 2026-10-23 gpt-4-0613 | gpt-4 , gpt-4-0613-completions , gpt-4-completions gpt-5.5 [...] 2026-10-23 o4-mini-2025-04-16 | o4-mini gpt-5.4-mini

events.headline: OpenAI phases out self-serve fine-tuning
events.summary: OpenAI is winding down the self-serve fine-tuning platform in stages: from May 7, 2026 organizations that never ran fine-tuning cannot start; from July 2, 2026 job creation requires recent fine-tuned inference; from January 6, 2027 even active customers cannot create new jobs. Inference on existing fine-tuned models continues until each underlying base model is deprecated.

sources.quote: On May 7th, 2026, we notified developers using OpenAI's self-serve fine-tuning platform of updates to availability. Inference on fine-tuned models will continue to be available until the base models are deprecated.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates gpt-5.2-chat-latest and gpt-5.3-chat-latest
events.summary: The gpt-5.2 and gpt-5.3 chat-latest snapshots were deprecated May 8, 2026 and shut down August 10, 2026. Recommended replacement: gpt-5.5.

sources.quote: On May 8th, 2026, we notified developers using gpt-5.2-chat-latest and gpt-5.3-chat-latest model snapshots of their deprecation and removal from the API.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates the GPT Image 1 family, shutdown December 2026
events.summary: gpt-image-1-mini, gpt-image-1.5, and chatgpt-image-latest were deprecated June 2, 2026 and shut down December 1, 2026. Recommended replacement: gpt-image-2.

sources.quote: On June 2, 2026, we notified developers using older GPT Image models of their deprecation and removal from the API on December 1, 2026.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates reusable prompt objects and the v1/prompts API
events.summary: Reusable prompt objects were deprecated June 3, 2026 with prompt creation de-emphasized immediately; the v1/prompts API shuts down November 30, 2026. Migration: move reusable prompt content into application code.

sources.quote: On June 3, 2026, we notified developers using reusable prompts in the dashboard and API that reusable prompt objects are being deprecated.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates the Evals platform
events.summary: The Evals platform was deprecated June 3, 2026; existing evals become read-only October 31, 2026 and the dashboard and API shut down November 30, 2026. OpenAI points to Promptfoo as a migration path.

sources.quote: On June 3, 2026, we notified developers using the Evals platform that the product is being deprecated.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates Agent Builder
events.summary: Agent Builder was deprecated June 3, 2026 and shuts down November 30, 2026. ChatKit remains available; OpenAI's migration guide points to the Agents SDK or ChatGPT Workspace Agents.

sources.quote: On June 3, 2026, we notified developers using Agent Builder that the product is being deprecated. ChatKit remains available.

events.headline: OpenAI deprecates original GPT-5 and o3 snapshots, shutdown December 2026
events.summary: The original GPT-5 snapshots (gpt-5, mini, nano, pro) and the o3/o3-pro snapshots were deprecated June 11, 2026 and shut down December 11, 2026. Replacements per model: gpt-5.5, gpt-5.4-mini, gpt-5.4-nano, and gpt-5.5-pro.

sources.quote: On June 11, 2026, we notified developers using older GPT-5 and o3 model snapshots of their deprecation and removal from the API on December 11, 2026.

events.headline: Anthropic retires the Claude 1 and Claude Instant model families
events.summary: All Claude 1 and Claude Instant models were deprecated September 4, 2024 and retired November 6, 2024. The deprecations page currently lists claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 as the recommended replacement (a retroactive update — that model postdates the announcement).

sources.quote: On September 4, 2024, Anthropic notified developers using Claude 1 and Instant models of their upcoming retirements.

events.headline: Anthropic retires Claude 2, Claude 2.1, and Claude Sonnet 3
events.summary: claude-2.0, claude-2.1, and claude-3-sonnet-20240229 were deprecated January 21, 2025 and retired July 21, 2025. Recommended replacements per the page: claude-opus-4-8 for the Claude 2 models, claude-sonnet-4-6 for Sonnet 3.

sources.quote: On January 21, 2025, Anthropic notified developers using Claude 2, Claude 2.1, and Claude Sonnet 3 models of their upcoming retirements.

events.headline: Anthropic deprecates Claude Opus 3, retired January 2026
events.summary: claude-3-opus-20240229 was deprecated June 30, 2025 and retired January 5, 2026. Recommended replacement: claude-opus-4-8.

sources.quote: On June 30, 2025, Anthropic notified developers using Claude Opus 3 model of its upcoming retirement.

events.headline: Anthropic deprecates both Claude Sonnet 3.5 snapshots, retired October 2025
events.summary: claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620 and claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022 were deprecated August 13, 2025 and retired October 28, 2025. Recommended replacement: claude-sonnet-4-6.

sources.quote: On August 13, 2025, Anthropic notified developers using Claude Sonnet 3.5 models of their upcoming retirement.

events.headline: Anthropic deprecates Claude Sonnet 3.7, retired February 2026
events.summary: claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219 was deprecated October 28, 2025 and retired February 19, 2026. Recommended replacement: claude-sonnet-4-6.

sources.quote: On October 28, 2025, Anthropic notified developers using Claude Sonnet 3.7 model of its upcoming retirement on the Claude API.

events.headline: Anthropic deprecates Claude Haiku 3.5, retired February 2026
events.summary: claude-3-5-haiku-20241022 was deprecated December 19, 2025 and retired February 19, 2026. Recommended replacement: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001.

sources.quote: On December 19, 2025, Anthropic notified developers using Claude Haiku 3.5 model of its upcoming retirement on the Claude API.

events.headline: Anthropic deprecates Claude Haiku 3, retired April 2026
events.summary: claude-3-haiku-20240307 was deprecated February 19, 2026 and retired April 20, 2026. Recommended replacement: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001.

sources.quote: On February 19, 2026, Anthropic notified developers using Claude Haiku 3 model of its upcoming retirement on the Claude API.

events.headline: Anthropic deprecates temperature, top_p, and top_k parameters on Claude Opus 4.7 and later
events.summary: Setting temperature, top_p, or top_k to a non-default value returns a 400 error on Claude Opus 4.7 and later, including Claude Opus 4.8 and Claude Sonnet 5. The parameters remain in SDK request types so existing code type-checks. Recommended approach: omit them and guide behavior via prompting. The page does not date this change; announced_at reflects the date it was captured from the page.

sources.quote: temperature , top_p , top_k Deprecated (Claude Opus 4.7 and later) Returns a 400 error when set to a non-default value on Claude Opus 4.7 and later, including Claude Opus 4.8, and on Claude Sonnet 5. Omit and use prompting to guide model behavior.

events.headline: xAI retires Grok 3, Grok 4-era, and grok-code-fast models effective May 15, 2026
events.summary: xAI retired eight models from its API effective May 15, 2026 at 12:00 PM PT. Recommended replacements per the migration guide: grok-4.3 (at varying reasoning-effort levels) for the Grok 3/4 text models, grok-build-0.1 for grok-code-fast-1, and grok-imagine-image-quality for grok-imagine-image-pro. The migration page itself is undated; announced_at reflects the earliest archive.org capture of the page (2026-05-07).

sources.quote: As we continue advancing Grok, we are retiring several earlier models to focus fully on our newest generation. Effective May 15, 2026 at 12:00 PM PT , the following models will be retired from the xAI API:

events.headline: xAI silently redirects retired Grok model slugs to grok-4.3 at grok-4.3 pricing
events.summary: From May 15, 2026, requests to the retired Grok slugs do not fail — they automatically redirect to grok-4.3 (grok-code-fast-1 redirects to grok-build-0.1) and are billed at the new model's rates ($1.25/$2.50 per 1M tokens) instead of the original model's. Code keeps working while the model and the price underneath it change — the exact stable-ID model swap the record exists to flag.

sources.quote: After May 15, 2026 at 12:00 PM PT , requests to the retired model slugs above will automatically redirect to grok-4.3 . The slugs themselves continue to resolve, so you do not need to change your code to avoid breakage. Grok 4.3 is priced differently than the various models it replaces.
