sources.quote: Claude 3.7 Sonnet is deprecated as of 2025-11-11 and will be shut down on 2026-05-11 ... Model ID claude-3-7-sonnet

events.headline: Vertex AI retires Claude 3.5 Haiku (shutdown 2026-07-05)
events.summary: Google Vertex AI deprecated Claude 3.5 Haiku as of 2026-01-05, with shutdown on 2026-07-05; deprecated models are available to existing customers only. Vertex (MaaS partner-model) dates are platform-specific and may differ from the model provider's own schedule.

sources.quote: Claude 3.5 Haiku is deprecated as of 2026-01-05 and will be shut down on 2026-07-05 ... Model ID claude-3-5-haiku

events.headline: Vertex AI retires Anthropic's Claude 3 Haiku (shutdown 2026-08-23)
events.summary: Google Vertex AI deprecated Anthropic's Claude 3 Haiku as of 2026-02-23, with shutdown on 2026-08-23; deprecated models are available to existing customers only. Vertex (MaaS partner-model) dates are platform-specific and may differ from the model provider's own schedule.

sources.quote: Anthropic's Claude 3 Haiku is deprecated as of 2026-02-23 and will be shut down on 2026-08-23 ... Model ID claude-3-haiku

events.headline: Anthropic in discussions with Samsung to develop custom AI chip
events.summary: Anthropic is exploring a partnership with Samsung to design and manufacture a custom AI chip, aiming to optimize performance and reduce dependency on existing GPU suppliers. This follows OpenAI's custom chip collaboration with Broadcom, part of a trend of major AI firms seeking greater hardware control.

events.headline: Nvidia software optimizations reduce DeepSeek V4 token costs by up to fivefold through Blackwell tuning
events.summary: One month after DeepSeek V4 launched, Nvidia shipped pure software optimizations on Blackwell achieving up to a 5x reduction in token generation costs — an inference-efficiency gain with no model or hardware changes. Efficiency news, not a provider price change: DeepSeek's posted API rates were not altered by this.

events.headline: Keling AI raises nearly $3 billion in second-largest single round after DeepSeek
events.summary: Keling AI has secured approximately $3 billion in a recent financing round, marking one of the largest AI-focused fundraisings to date. This positions Keling as a major contender in the competitive AI landscape, following DeepSeek's record round.

events.headline: Claude Fable 5 from Anthropic becomes available on Amazon Web Services.
events.summary: Anthropic's new model, Claude Fable 5, is now accessible via AWS, expanding its deployment options and integration within the Amazon cloud ecosystem. This enhances availability for enterprise users and signals a strategic cloud partnership.

events.headline: Anthropic calls for AI regulation following White House loosening of restrictions on cyber-capable models
events.summary: In early July 2026, Anthropic advocated for increased AI regulation after the White House eased constraints on AI models with cyber capabilities. The company expressed concerns about potential misuse and emphasized the need for responsible deployment frameworks.

events.headline: US lifts export control restrictions on Anthropic, enabling Claude Fable 5's return to international markets
events.summary: The United States has lifted export control restrictions that previously limited Anthropic's ability to distribute Claude Fable 5 internationally. This regulatory change allows Anthropic to resume offering the model globally, reversing prior availability constraints imposed by national security or foreign policy concerns.

events.headline: Priority scheduling now limited to text inference endpoints
events.summary: The service_tier parameter with 'priority' is now only applicable to text inference endpoints (Chat Completions and Responses), no longer supporting image and video endpoints.

sources.quote: You can now request higher scheduling priority per request by setting service_tier: "priority" on text inference endpoints (Chat Completions and Responses). The response's service_tier field reports the tier actually applied, and priority rates are billed only when priority is used. For more details, see the Priority Processing docs .

events.headline: DeepSeek in development of custom AI chip to enhance inference efficiency
events.summary: Citing internal sources, Reuters reports that DeepSeek is designing its own AI chip to reduce reliance on third-party hardware and optimize performance for its models, particularly DeepSeek-V4 and future iterations. This move signals a strategic push toward full-stack AI development, potentially lowering operational costs and improving scalability.

events.headline: Lightworks, Scotiabank, Sun Life, and TELUS launch Canadian AI Consortium to build AI control infrastructure
events.summary: A coalition of Lightworks, Scotiabank, Sun Life, and TELUS has formed an AI Consortium focused on developing critical AI control infrastructure in Canada. This initiative aims to establish secure, compliant, and scalable frameworks for AI deployment across sectors, with implications for model governance, access controls, and national AI policy alignment.

events.headline: xAI officially rebrands as SpaceXAI five months after merger
events.summary: Following its acquisition by SpaceX, xAI has formally changed its name to SpaceXAI, signaling full integration into the aerospace company. This marks a major consolidation in the AI sector, aligning advanced AI development with SpaceX's long-term technological goals.

events.headline: MiniMax models become available on Amazon Bedrock
events.summary: MiniMax's Abori and Abori Pro models are now accessible via Amazon Bedrock, enabling AWS customers to leverage these models for on-demand inference with scalable APIs. This integration expands Bedrock's multimodal and reasoning capabilities to international markets.

events.headline: GPT-Realtime-2.1 and GPT-Realtime-2.1 mini launched
events.summary: OpenAI released GPT-Realtime-2.1, an updated realtime reasoning model with improved alphanumeric recognition, silence and noise handling, and interruption behavior. Additionally, GPT-Realtime-2.1 mini was released as a faster, lower-cost distilled version for realtime voice applications.

sources.quote: Released GPT-Realtime-2.1 , an updated realtime reasoning model with improved alphanumeric recognition, silence and noise handling, and interruption behavior. Also released GPT-Realtime-2.1 mini , a faster, lower-cost distilled reasoning model for realtime voice applications.

events.headline: Nous Research releases Yarn-Mistral-7B-128K, extending Mistral 7B to 128K context
events.summary: Nous Research publishes Yarn-Mistral-7b-128k (Apache-2.0), a Mistral-7B model further pretrained with the YaRN method to a 128K-token context window. YaRN (arXiv:2309.00071) becomes a widely adopted technique for efficient context-window extension.

sources.quote: Nous-Yarn-Mistral-7b-128k is a state-of-the-art language model for long context, further pretrained on long context data

events.headline: Nous Research releases Nous Hermes 2 - Yi-34B
events.summary: Nous Research releases Nous-Hermes-2-Yi-34B (Apache-2.0), a fine-tune of 01.AI's Yi-34B trained on 1,000,000 primarily GPT-4-generated entries. An early entry in the Nous Hermes 2 generation.

sources.quote: Nous Hermes 2 - Yi-34B is a state of the art Yi Fine-tune

events.headline: Nous Research releases Genstruct-7B, an instruction-generation model
events.summary: Nous Research releases Genstruct-7B (Apache-2.0), a Mistral-7B-based model that generates valid instructions from a raw-text corpus, enabling creation of partially synthetic instruction-tuning datasets. Inspired by Ada-Instruct, grounding generations in user-provided context passages.

sources.quote: Genstruct 7B is an instruction-generation model, designed to create valid instructions given a raw text corpus

events.headline: Nous Research releases Nous Hermes 2 - Mixtral 8x7B (DPO + SFT)
events.summary: Nous Research releases Nous-Hermes-2-Mixtral-8x7B (Apache-2.0), its first flagship built on Mistral's Mixtral 8x7B mixture-of-experts model, trained on over 1,000,000 entries. Published in both DPO and SFT variants.

sources.quote: Nous Hermes 2 Mixtral 8x7B DPO is the new flagship Nous Research model trained over the Mixtral 8x7B MoE LLM

events.headline: Nous Research releases Hermes 2 Pro - Mistral 7B with function calling and JSON mode
events.summary: Nous Research releases Hermes-2-Pro-Mistral-7B (Apache-2.0), a retrained Nous Hermes 2 on a cleaned OpenHermes 2.5 dataset plus a new function-calling and JSON-mode dataset - establishing the Hermes line's structured tool-use capability.

sources.quote: Hermes 2 Pro on Mistral 7B is the new flagship 7B Hermes!

events.headline: Nous Research releases Hermes 2 Theta - Llama-3 70B
events.summary: Nous Research releases Hermes-2-Theta-Llama-3-70B (Llama 3 license), an experimental merged model built on Meta Llama 3 70B in collaboration with Charles Goddard and Arcee AI.

sources.quote: Hermes-2 Θ (Theta) 70B is the continuation of our experimental merged model released by Nous Research, in collaboration with Charles Goddard and Arcee AI

events.headline: Nous Research releases Hermes 3 on Llama 3.1 (8B, 70B, 405B)
events.summary: Nous Research releases the Hermes 3 generation (Llama 3 license) across 8B, 70B, and flagship 405B sizes - its first full-parameter finetune of Meta Llama-3.1-405B. Hermes 3 405B is hosted by resellers including Venice and OpenRouter.

sources.quote: Hermes 3 405B is the latest flagship model in the Hermes series of LLMs by Nous Research, and the first full parameter finetune since the release of Llama-3.1 405B

events.headline: Nous Research releases DeepHermes 3, unifying reasoning and normal response modes
events.summary: Nous Research releases DeepHermes-3-Llama-3-8B-Preview (Llama 3 license), one of the first models to unify long chain-of-thought reasoning and traditional instruct responses in a single model, toggled by a system prompt. Larger 3B and 24B previews followed.

sources.quote: one of the first models in the world to unify Reasoning (long chains of thought that improve answer accuracy) and normal LLM response modes into one model

events.headline: Nous Research releases Hermes 4, a hybrid-mode reasoning model on Llama 3.1 405B
events.summary: Nous Research releases Hermes 4 (Llama 3 license), a frontier hybrid-mode reasoning model built on Llama-3.1-405B, with 70B and 14B sizes following in August. Adds a synthesized post-training corpus emphasizing verified reasoning traces, with gains in math, code, STEM, and format-faithful output. Available via resellers including OpenRouter.

sources.quote: Hermes 4 405B is a frontier, hybrid-mode reasoning model based on Llama-3.1-405B by Nous Research

events.headline: Nous Research releases Hermes 4.3 (36B), its first decentralized-trained model
events.summary: Nous Research releases Hermes-4.3-36B (Apache-2.0), a frontier hybrid-mode reasoning model based on ByteDance's Seed-OSS-36B base - the first Hermes model trained in a decentralized manner over the internet using the Psyche network.

sources.quote: This is our first Hermes model trained in a decentralized manner over the internet using Psyche

events.headline: Nous Research releases Nomos 1 for mathematical proof-writing
events.summary: Nous Research releases Nomos-1 (Apache-2.0), a specialization of Qwen3-30B-A3B-Thinking-2507 for mathematical problem-solving and proof-writing in natural language, trained with Hillclimb AI. Scores 87/120 on Putnam 2025; ships with the open-source Nomos Reasoning Harness.

sources.quote: We release Nomos 1, a specialization of Qwen/Qwen3-30B-A3B-Thinking-2507 for mathematical problem-solving and proof-writing in natural language

events.headline: Nous Research releases NousCoder-14B for competitive programming
events.summary: Nous Research releases NousCoder-14B (Apache-2.0), a competitive-programming model post-trained on Qwen3-14B via reinforcement learning over 24k verifiable coding problems. Reaches 67.87% Pass@1 on LiveCodeBench v6, up 7.08 points over the Qwen3-14B baseline.

sources.quote: a competitive programming model post-trained on Qwen3-14B via reinforcement learning

events.headline: SambaNova raises $1B at $11B valuation in Series F first close.
events.summary: AI chip maker SambaNova secured $1B in funding at an $11B valuation, a significant increase from Intel's rumored $1.6B acquisition bid months prior. This round underscores strong investor confidence in SambaNova's position in the competitive AI hardware market.

events.headline: D-Matrix launches Corsair AI inference platform to challenge Nvidia's GPU dominance
events.summary: D-Matrix has launched the Corsair AI inference platform, positioning it as a competitive alternative to Nvidia's dominant GPU-based AI acceleration stack. The platform aims to deliver high-efficiency inference capabilities for large language models and other AI workloads, signaling a new entrant in the foundational AI infrastructure space.

events.headline: Nvidia launches $27B 'AI factory' initiative partnering with chip rivals to scale AI compute infrastructure
events.summary: Under Jensen Huang's 'AI factory' strategy, Nvidia commits $27 billion to partner with competing chip firms to dramatically expand AI compute capacity. This initiative aims to standardize and scale the production of AI hardware infrastructure globally.

events.headline: Together AI launches Provisioned Throughput for reserved inference capacity on open models
events.summary: Together AI introduces Provisioned Throughput, a new offering that provides reserved inference capacity for frontier open models like MiniMax M3 and GLM-5.2. It features token-based pricing, a 99% uptime SLA, and costs up to 90% less than proprietary APIs, eliminating GPU-hour calculations and infrastructure management.

events.headline: Expanded lawsuit alleges xAI's Grok enabled generation of deepfake child sexual abuse material
events.summary: A growing legal action against xAI claims its Grok AI model was used to create and distribute deepfake child sexual exploitation content, raising serious concerns about model misuse, content moderation, and corporate liability. The lawsuit could prompt regulatory scrutiny and force changes in model safeguards or deployment policies.

events.headline: xAI (SpaceXAI) makes Grok 4.5 publicly available
events.summary: xAI, now branded SpaceXAI, opens Grok 4.5 to public access, following the model's late-June launch and private beta at SpaceX and Tesla. Reported by multiple outlets as an 'Opus-class' model positioned against Anthropic's Claude Opus.

events.headline: Claude Sonnet 5 launched with 1M token context window and introductory pricing
events.summary: Claude Sonnet 5 is now available with a 1M token context window, 128k max output tokens, and introductory pricing of $2/$10 per MTok until August 31, 2026, after which standard pricing of $3/$15 applies. Priority Tier is not available on this model.

sources.quote: We've launched Claude Sonnet 5 ( claude-sonnet-5 ), the next generation of our Sonnet model family, at introductory pricing of $2 / $10 per MTok through August 31, 2026 (standard $3 / $15 thereafter). Claude Sonnet 5 supports a 1M token context window , 128k max output tokens, and the same set of tools and platform features as Claude Sonnet 4.6, except Priority Tier , which is not available on Claude Sonnet 5.

events.headline: Adaptive thinking enabled by default; manual extended thinking removed in Claude Sonnet 5
events.summary: In Claude Sonnet 5, adaptive thinking is now on by default. Manual extended thinking is removed and returns a 400 error. Setting sampling parameters (temperature, top_p, top_k) to non-default values also returns a 400 error.

sources.quote: Three behavior changes apply when migrating: adaptive thinking is now on by default; manual extended thinking ( thinking: {type: "enabled", budget_tokens: N} ) is removed and returns a 400 error (it was deprecated on Sonnet 4.6); and setting sampling parameters ( temperature , top_p , top_k ) to non-default values returns a 400 error.

events.headline: Claude Sonnet 5 uses new tokenizer producing ~30% more tokens
events.summary: Claude Sonnet 5 uses a new tokenizer that generates approximately 30% more tokens for the same text, varying by content and workload.

sources.quote: Claude Sonnet 5 also uses a new tokenizer that produces approximately 30% more tokens for the same text. The exact increase depends on the content and workload shape.

events.headline: MAI-Image-2e (Azure Foundry) to be retired on 2026-08-15
events.summary: Microsoft's MAI-Image-2e image model (Preview on Microsoft Foundry, version 2026-04-09) is scheduled for retirement on 2026-08-15, with MAI-Image-2.5-Flash named as the replacement, per the Foundry model retirement schedule.

sources.quote: MAI-Image-2e | 2026-04-09 | Preview | 2026-08-15 | MAI-Image-2.5-Flash

events.headline: MAI-Image-2 (Azure Foundry) to be retired on 2026-08-15
events.summary: Microsoft's MAI-Image-2 image model (Preview on Microsoft Foundry, version 2026-02-20) is scheduled for retirement on 2026-08-15, with MAI-Image-2.5 named as the replacement, per the Foundry model retirement schedule.

sources.quote: MAI-Image-2 | 2026-02-20 | Preview | 2026-08-15 | MAI-Image-2.5

events.headline: GPT-5.6 adds new capabilities
events.summary: GPT-5.6 introduces programmatic tool calling, explicit prompt caching controls, persisted reasoning, max reasoning effort, Pro mode, multi-agent orchestration in beta for the Responses API, and support for images at original dimensions with original or auto image detail.

sources.quote: GPT-5.6 adds Programmatic Tool Calling , explicit prompt caching controls , persisted reasoning, max reasoning effort, and Pro mode , and Multi-agent orchestration in beta for the Responses API . GPT-5.6 also accepts images at their original dimensions with original or auto image detail.

events.headline: Amazon Bedrock moves Claude Opus 4.1 to Legacy (EOL 2027-01-08)
events.summary: Amazon Bedrock moved Claude Opus 4.1 (anthropic.claude-opus-4-1-20250805-v1:0) to the Legacy state on 2026-07-08 in us-east-1, us-east-2, and us-west-2, with an end-of-life date of 2027-01-08. Public extended access (at potentially higher, provider-set pricing) begins 2026-10-08. New customers cannot start using a Legacy model.

sources.quote: Claude Opus 4.1 | anthropic.claude-opus-4-1-20250805-v1:0 | Legacy date: July 8, 2026 | EOL date: January 8, 2027 | Public extended access start date: October 8, 2026

events.headline: Ollama raises $65M and reaches nearly 9M users
events.summary: Ollama, a leading open-source tool for running AI models locally, has secured $65M in funding and grown its user base to nearly 9 million. The platform has gained widespread developer adoption, evidenced by 176,000 GitHub stars and 17,000 forks, reinforcing its role in decentralized AI inference.

events.headline: HUMAIN and Cohere launch strategic sovereign AI compute collaboration
events.summary: HUMAIN and Cohere have partnered to develop sovereign AI compute solutions, aiming to establish regionally controlled AI infrastructure with localized data governance and model deployment. This collaboration emphasizes data residency and compliance for African markets.

events.headline: New agent-memory-2026-07-22 beta header changes memory listing behavior
events.summary: The agent-memory-2026-07-22 beta header modifies how memory listing results are returned, enforcing a stable server-defined order, restricting depth values, and changing path_prefix matching behavior. It replaces the managed-agents-2026-04-01 header for memory store calls.

sources.quote: We've added the agent-memory-2026-07-22 beta header, which changes how listing memories ( GET /v1/memory_stores/{memory_store_id}/memories ) behaves: results are returned in a stable, server-defined order and the order_by and order parameters are ignored; depth accepts only 0 , 1 , or being omitted (other values return a 400 error); and path_prefix must end with / and matches whole path segments instead of a substring. Page cursors issued without the header aren't valid with it, so restart from the first page when you adopt it. On memory store endpoints, agent-memory-2026-07-22 replaces managed-agents-2026-04-01 ; sending both returns a 400 error. On July 22, 2026, the managed-agents-2026-04-01 header adopts the same list behavior. See Beta headers .

events.headline: The New York Times files motion for sanctions alleging OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
events.summary: The New York Times and other publishers allege that OpenAI concealed tools and datasets that could trace copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their ongoing copyright lawsuit with a motion for sanctions. This legal action highlights growing tensions over data transparency and intellectual property in AI model training.

events.headline: U.S. seeks to restrict Chinese AI models in corporate use, but open-weight models undermine enforcement
events.summary: The U.S. government is pushing to exclude Chinese AI models from corporate America due to security and geopolitical concerns. However, the proliferation of open-weight models enables continued access and deployment despite regulatory intent, limiting the effectiveness of such restrictions.

events.headline: MiniMax raises $2.05B for record open-weight AI model development
events.summary: MiniMax has secured $2.05B in funding to develop a record-scale open-weight AI model, marking one of the largest financings in the AI sector this year. This investment positions MiniMax as a major player in the open-model ecosystem and could accelerate the release of high-capacity models with broad accessibility.

events.headline: 100 authors file $75M lawsuit against Anthropic for alleged unauthorized use of copyrighted works in model training
events.summary: A group of 100 authors has filed a lawsuit demanding $75 million in damages from Anthropic, alleging that the company used their copyrighted works without permission to train its AI systems. The suit raises critical questions about intellectual property rights in AI development and could set legal precedents for data sourcing in the industry.

events.headline: Organization and project spend limits added
events.summary: OpenAI has introduced spend limits for organizations and projects on its API platform, allowing users to set monthly budgets to monitor costs or enforce hard limits that cause API calls to fail once the limit is reached.

sources.quote: Added organization and project spend limits for the OpenAI API platform. Set monthly spend limits to monitor API costs, or enforce hard limits so API responses fail after an organization or project reaches its limit. Read more in the spend limits guide .

events.headline: Grok 4.5 launched for EU users
events.summary: Grok 4.5 is now accessible to EU users via the API console, expanding regional availability.

sources.quote: Grok 4.5 is now available in the API console for EU users. See the Grok 4.5 overview .

events.headline: Fireworks models on Standard inference now subject to 15-day retirement notice
events.summary: Microsoft has updated its policy to require only a 15-day notice period for the retirement of Fireworks models on Standard (Per-Token) inference offerings, replacing the previous longer notice period.

sources.quote: Fireworks models on Standard (Per-Token) inference offerings are subject to a 15-day notice period prior to model retirement. Plan your deployments accordingly and monitor notifications for upcoming retirement dates.
